description_long: This object is a vintage-style stereo sound system with a rectangular, dark wood cabinet and a classic design. The front features a decorative speaker grille with three vertical slats and a woven mesh background, giving it an art deco appearance. Below the grille is a circular analog dial, likely for tuning, set within an octagonal frame, and flanked by two round control knobs. The sides and back are plain, finished in the same dark wood, with no visible openings or controls. The top is flat, with the grille slats extending slightly above the surface, adding a subtle architectural detail. The overall look is reminiscent of early 20th-century tabletop radios, blending functionality with decorative craftsmanship.
description: A vintage-style stereo sound system with a dark wood cabinet, vertical grille slats, and an analog dial with control knobs on the front. The design is classic and decorative, evoking early radio aesthetics.
description_view_0: The left side view shows a plain, flat dark wood panel with no visible features or controls.
description_view_1: The front view displays the decorative speaker grille with three vertical slats, a woven mesh, an analog dial, and two round control knobs.
description_view_2: The right side view mirrors the left, showing a plain, flat dark wood surface without any controls or openings.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals a simple, unadorned dark wood panel, with the tops of the grille slats visible above the surface.
attribute_speaker_grille_pattern: Three vertical rectangular slats over a woven mesh grille, forming a symmetrical art deco pattern.
attribute_control_panel_layout: Two round control knobs and a central analog dial located below the speaker grille, with no visible labels.
